Eagle Strafing Run

From Helldivers Wiki
Jump to navigation Jump to search
  Helldivers 2   Helldivers 1    
Last updated: 1.001.104
Current patch: 1.006.300

Stratagems
Hangar
Offensive
Eagle
Eagle Strafing Run
Eagle Strafing Run
Stratagem Details
Permit Type
Offensive
Traits
Eagle  •  Anti-Tank
Stratagem Code
Base Cooldown
15s
Procurement
Unlock Level
1
Unlock Cost
1500 Requisition Slips
Source
Hangar
“

A strafing run of the battlefield to clear small targets, delivered almost instantly. When called the strike will start from the beacon and go away from the direction you were facing when thrown

— Ship Management Terminal

The Eagle Strafing Run is an offensive stratagem that calls the Eagle in to fire a barrage of anti-tank, high-explosive shells, parallel to the player.

Contents

  • 1 Stratagem Statistics
  • 2 Detailed Weapon Statistics
  • 3 Tactical Information
  • 4 Applicable Ship Modules
  • 5 Media
  • 6 Trivia
  • 7 Change History
  • 8 References

Stratagem Statistics

General
Call-in Time 2.4 seconds
Rearm Time Standard 150 seconds
With Pit Crew Hazard Pay 120 seconds
With Advanced Crew Training 108 seconds
Uses Standard 4
Upgraded 5
Cooldown Standard 15 seconds
With Morale Augmentation 14.25 seconds
With Liquid-Ventilated Cockpit 7.5 seconds
With All Upgrades 6.75 seconds


Detailed Weapon Statistics

Eagle Strafing Run
Cooldown 15 s
Uses 4
Main Health 100
Main Armor  Unarmored I

Main Health 100
Main Armor  Unarmored I

Recoil 0

Sway 1

Outer Radius 349 - 0
Outer Durable 349 - 0
Penetration
Direct  Medium
AoE Effect
Demolition Force 30
Stagger Force 35
Push Force 5

Tactical Information

  • This stratagem can effectively clear small to medium-sized enemies and damage or even kill Heavies, although the latter is inconsistent against ones of smaller size.
    • Helldivers may want to rely on other, more consistent stratagems such as the Eagle 500kg Bomb or dedicated anti-tank support weapons to deal with Heavies.
    • For best results when using the Strafing Run against Heavies, line up the strategem beacon toward any weak spots (such as a burrowed Impaler's face) or along the length of the enemy.
      • This can even be done to destroy a Leviathan in one or two uses of the strategem, so long as it is flying low enough. The strategem beacon should be thrown from underneath the front or back of the Leviathan to the opposite end, so that the shells hit it on their way to the target area on the ground.
  • The stratagem has an apparent maximum range of 50m, beginning from its stratagem beacon. Its effective range is dependent on the surrounding terrain.
  • The Eagle Strafing Run fires a burst of 100 rounds per use, with 25 of the rounds being high-explosive.
  • Out of all the Eagle stratagems, the Strafing Run deploys the quickest. This is most likely due to the shells traveling faster than rockets and bombs from the fighter craft.
  • The Strafing Run fires in the direction you throw the Stratagem. Throw behind yourself while being chased for maximum effect (bouncing the Stratagem may change the Strafe direction).
    • The stratagem beacon's direction is chosen based on where the beacon lands and where you throw it from. A bouncing beacon makes it harder to keep track of the direction
  • Unlike most offensive Stratagems, the Eagle Strafing Run will not alert or aggro enemies until the first shot is fired.
  • The Strafing Run can destroy Automaton Fabricators, Enemy Bio-Processors, AA & Mortar Emplacements, grounded Dropships, Spore Spewers, Shrieker Nests and Illegal Broadcast Towers.
    • The Strafing Run can possibly destroy Bug Holes if a shell lands inside. This can be more consistently achieved with some Bug Hole variants by throwing the stratagem beacon directly in front of or on the lip to the opening; some variants, often ones with "roofs" or other top-covering obstacles, will be more difficult to destroy consistently.
    • A single Strafing Run cannot destroy a shielded grounded Illuminate Warp Ship, but will break the shield, allowing a follow-up Strafing Run or explosive to finish the job. Alternatively, the shield can be stripped by other means, and a single Strafing Run used to destroy the Warp Ship.
  • The Strafing Run deals massive damage against Hive Lords due to the large size of the beast. It is recommended to throw the strafing run as close to the Hive Lord as possible to ensure maximum hits.

Applicable Ship Modules

The following Ship Modules affect the Eagle Strafing Run:

Department Ship Module Effect
Hangar Liquid-Ventilated Cockpit Reduces Eagle stratagem cooldown time by 50%.
Pit Crew Hazard Pay Reduces Eagle Rearm time by 20%.
Expanded Weapons Bay Increases the number of Eagle stratagem uses per Rearm by 1.
Advanced Crew Training Eagle Rearm further reduced by 10% if called in while Eagle uses still remain.
Bridge Morale Augmentation Reduces cooldown time for all Stratagems by 5%.

Media

Tutorial Video Demonstrating Use of the Eagle Strafing Run

Trivia

  • During development, the statistics of the Eagle Strafing Run were modeled after the GAU-8 Avenger Gatling cannon famously used on the A-10 Thunderbolt, but it caused severe lag due to the high fire rate and explosive ammunition fired every 5th shot.[1]

Change History

Patch
Description

1.001.104
2024-10-15

Changes

  • Explosion radius slightly increased
    • inner radius increased from 1.5m to 2.5m and outer radius from 4.5m to 5m
  • Explosion damage increased from 250 to 350

1.001.100
2024-09-17

Changes

  • Every Stratagem capable of damaging heavily armored enemies has had their damage increased to account for the increased health of heavier enemies.
    • Projectile standard damage increased from 300 to 350.
    • Projectile durable damage increased from 100 to 200.

1.000.400
2024-06-13

Changes

  • Increased uses from 3 to 4.
  • Increased armor penetration, can now damage heavily armored enemies.
    • Direct Penetration increased from  Heavy to  Anti-Tank I.
    • Slight Angle Penetration increased from  Medium to  Anti-Tank I.
    • Large Angle Penetration increased from  Light to  Heavy.

Undocumented Changes

  • Bullet Durable damage reduced from 150 to 100.
  • Bullet Stagger Force increased from 30 to 35.
  • Explosion Stagger Force decreased from 40 to 35.
  • Explosion Push Force decreased from 10 to 5.

References

  1. ↑ ""Fun fact, originally the eagle strafing run had the exact stats of the A10 GAU-8, but it made the game lag too much due to the fire rate and 1-in-5 explosive rounds."" @Pilestedt on Xby Pilestedt. Published on January 3, 2025.
